description:
The Counter Terrorism Command (SO15), part of the Metropolitan Police Service, is the UK's lead police unit for terrorism investigations. Formed in October 2006 through the merger of the Anti-Terrorist Branch (SO13) and Special Branch (SO12), SO15 has more than 1,500 officers and staff. It works in close partnership with MI5 and MI6 and leads investigations into state-sponsored assassination plots targeting individuals on UK soil.

SO15 led the investigation into the March 2024 stabbing of [Iran International](/orgs/iran-international) journalist [Pouria Zeraati](/people/pouria-zeraati) outside his Wimbledon home, treating it as a potential state-directed attack. In May 2025 SO15 arrested and charged [Mostafa Sepahvand](/people/mostafa-sepahvand), [Farhad Javadi Manesh](/people/farhad-javadi-manesh), and [Shapoor Qalehali Khani Noori](/people/shapoor-qalehali-khani-noori) under the [National Security Act 2023 (UK)](/events/national-security-act-2023-uk) for conducting surveillance and reconnaissance against Iran International journalists on behalf of Iran's [Ministry of Intelligence](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence). This was the first prosecution of Iranian nationals under the National Security Act 2023. The three defendants face trial at the [Woolwich Crown Court](/events/woolwich-crown-court-iran-international-plot-2026) from 5 October 2026.

Since January 2022, SO15 and MI5 have responded to more than 20 Iran-backed plots presenting potentially lethal threats to individuals in the United Kingdom.

sources.title: Three men charged with National Security Act offences

editorial_notes: SO15 = Specialist Operations unit 15. Formed October 2006 from merger of SO13 (Anti-Terrorist Branch) and SO12 (Special Branch). Over 1,500 officers and staff. Works with MI5 and MI6.
